perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Termination for breach. A party may suspend performance and/or terminate this Agreement, with immediate effect, if the other party: (a) is in material breach of this Agreement where the breach is incapable of remedy; or (b) is in material breach of this Agreement where the breach is capable of remedy and fails to remedy that breach within thirty (30) days after receiving written notice of such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Termination for breach. A party may suspend performance and/or terminate this Agreement, with immediate effect, if the other party: (a) is in material breach of this Agreement where the breach is incapable of remedy; or (b) is in material breach of this Agreement where the breach is capable of remedy and fails to remedy that breach within sixty (60) days after receiving written notice of such breach.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction by changing the timeframe to remedy a breach. The original text grants thirty (30) days, but the modified text extends it to sixty (60) days. This creates uncertainty, as the contract now provides two distinct periods, raising disputes about the actual remedy timeline. This contradiction leads to ambiguity regarding which timeline should be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 Termination for breach. A party may suspend performance and/or terminate this Agreement, with immediate effect, if the other party: (a) is in material breach of this Agreement where the breach is incapable of remedy; or (b) is in material breach of this Agreement where the breach is capable of remedy and fails to remedy that breach within sixty (60) days after receiving written notice of such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the timeframe for remedying a breach from 30 days to 60 days. However, the contradicted text states the timeframe for remedying a breach is 60 days. Since they are not the same, there is a contradiction within the document. According to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties' which is what happens here.

perturbation.original_text: 5.6 Effect of Termination. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement: (a) all rights and licenses granted under this Agreement shall immediately cease; (b) Distributor shall (and shall ensure that any Third Party Distributors shall) immediately stop reproducing the Products and offering or distributing Bundles; (c) Distributor shall return or destroy (and a duly appointed officer of Distributor shall certify to such destruction) all copies of the Products and any other Google Confidential Information in its possession; and (d) the fees payable to Distributor shall immediately cease accruing and Google shall within [ * ] following such expiration or termination pay to Distributor any undisputed amounts which have accrued from the time of the most recent payment to Distributor through the date of termination or expiration of this Agreement. Clauses 5.6 (Effect of Termination), 6 (Confidential Information), 7 (Proprietary Rights), 9 (Limitation of Liability), 10 (Indemnification) and 11 (General) shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.6 Effect of Termination. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement: (a) all rights and licenses granted under this Agreement shall immediately cease; (b) Distributor shall (and shall ensure that any Third Party Distributors shall) immediately stop reproducing the Products and offering or distributing Bundles; (c) Distributor shall return or destroy (and a duly appointed officer of Distributor shall certify to such destruction) all copies of the Products and any other Google Confidential Information in its possession; and (d) the fees payable to Distributor shall immediately cease accruing and Google shall within 90 days following such expiration or termination pay to Distributor any undisputed amounts which have accrued from the time of the most recent payment to Distributor through the date of termination or expiration of this Agreement. Clauses 5.6 (Effect of Termination), 6 (Confidential Information), 7 (Proprietary Rights), 9 (Limitation of Liability), 10 (Indemnification) and 11 (General) shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification creates an in-text contradiction related to payment terms upon termination. The original text states Google should pay any undisputed amounts within a bracketed parameter [ * ] after termination. The changed text specifies 90 days. This introduces ambiguity since it's unclear whether the original unspecified timeline or the modified 90-day timeline should be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.6 Effect of Termination. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement: (a) all rights and licenses granted under this Agreement shall immediately cease; (b) Distributor shall (and shall ensure that any Third Party Distributors shall) immediately stop reproducing the Products and offering or distributing Bundles; (c) Distributor shall return or destroy (and a duly appointed officer of Distributor shall certify to such destruction) all copies of the Products and any other Google Confidential Information in its possession; and (d) the fees payable to Distributor shall immediately cease accruing and Google shall within 90 days following such expiration or termination pay to Distributor any undisputed amounts which have accrued from the time of the most recent payment to Distributor through the date of termination or expiration of this Agreement. Clauses 5.6 (Effect of Termination), 6 (Confidential Information), 7 (Proprietary Rights), 9 (Limitation of Liability), 10 (Indemnification) and 11 (General) shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the bracketed parameter '[ * ]' with '90 days' regarding when Google should pay undisputed amounts to the distributor upon termination. The original text leaves the payment timeframe unspecified which is then changed to 90 days. Because the contradicted text also shows '90 days', this means the changed text has now been applied to the contradictory text. This creates an inconsistency as the original text was ambiguous and now it has been rectified and made definitive. While the contradiction no longer exists, it did originally exist, therefore according to the instructions the answer is YES.
